questions.q: In the Puranas, Vaikuntha is described as the supreme celestial abode of which deity?
questions.explanation: Vaikuntha is described in the Puranas as the supreme celestial abode of Vishnu.

questions.q: Which goddess is said to dwell in Vaikuntha as the consort of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, the goddess of fortune, attends Vishnu in Vaikuntha as his consort.

questions.q: Upon which serpent is Vishnu said to recline in Vaikuntha?
questions.explanation: In Vaikuntha, Vishnu reclines upon the cosmic serpent Ananta Shesha.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, Vaikuntha lies beyond which kind of worlds?
questions.explanation: Vaikuntha is described as a realm beyond the material worlds, untouched by sorrow or decay.

questions.q: In Vaikuntha, Vishnu is said to recline upon the cosmic ocean of which substance?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclines on Ananta Shesha upon the cosmic ocean of milk, the Kshira Sagara.

questions.q: The Puranas describe Vaikuntha as a realm of eternal what?
questions.explanation: Vaikuntha is described in the Puranas as a realm of eternal bliss.

questions.q: Who are said to serve as the gatekeepers of Vaikuntha?
questions.explanation: Jaya and Vijaya are described in the Puranas as the gatekeepers of Vaikuntha.

questions.q: Vaikuntha is described as untouched by sorrow and what else?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Vaikuntha as untouched by sorrow or decay.

questions.q: In the Puranas, who are said to dwell eternally in Vaikuntha within Vishnu's grace?
questions.explanation: Liberated souls are said to dwell eternally in Vaikuntha in Vishnu's grace.

questions.q: Which deity, attended by Lakshmi, presides over the abode of Vaikuntha?
questions.explanation: Vishnu presides over Vaikuntha, attended by his consort Lakshmi.

questions.q: The serpent Ananta Shesha, on whom Vishnu reclines in Vaikuntha, is chiefly noted for having what feature?
questions.explanation: Ananta Shesha is depicted as a many-hooded cosmic serpent who shelters the reclining Vishnu.

questions.q: The cosmic ocean of milk on which Vishnu reclines in Vaikuntha is known by which Sanskrit name?
questions.explanation: The ocean of milk is called the Kshira Sagara in the Puranas.

questions.q: In Puranic accounts, the gatekeepers Jaya and Vijaya of Vaikuntha are cursed after barring which visitors?
questions.explanation: Jaya and Vijaya barred the four Kumara sages from Vaikuntha and were cursed to be born on earth.

questions.q: Which goddess, dwelling in Vaikuntha beside Vishnu, is associated with fortune and abundance?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, consort of Vishnu in Vaikuntha, is the goddess of fortune and abundance.

questions.q: The reclining form of Vishnu seen in Vaikuntha is most commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The reclining Vishnu upon Ananta Shesha is known as Anantashayana, or Padmanabha.

questions.q: Reaching Vaikuntha eternally is associated in the Puranas with which spiritual goal?
questions.explanation: Dwelling eternally in Vaikuntha is associated with liberation, or moksha, in Vishnu's grace.

questions.q: Which emblem, sacred to Vishnu, is most closely associated with the lotus-born imagery of Vaikuntha?
questions.explanation: The lotus is a central emblem of Vishnu, who is called Padmanabha, the lotus-naveled.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the lotus from which Brahma emerges is said to rise from the navel of which form of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: From the navel of the reclining Vishnu rises a lotus bearing Brahma, the creator.

questions.q: Which eagle, the vahana of Vishnu, is associated with serving the lord of Vaikuntha?
questions.explanation: Garuda, the divine eagle, is the vahana and devoted servant of Vishnu, lord of Vaikuntha.

questions.q: In Vaishnava tradition, Vaikuntha is most often contrasted as superior to which heavenly realm of Indra?
questions.explanation: Vaikuntha is held to surpass Svarga, Indra's heaven, which is still part of the material worlds subject to change.

questions.q: The conch held by Vishnu, lord of Vaikuntha, bears which well-known name?
questions.explanation: Panchajanya is the name of Vishnu's conch, while Sudarshana is his discus and Kaumodaki his mace.

questions.q: The discus carried by Vishnu, lord of Vaikuntha, is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's discus is the Sudarshana Chakra; the Vajra belongs to Indra and the Trishula to Shiva.

questions.q: Cursed at the gates of Vaikuntha, the gatekeepers Jaya and Vijaya are said in the Bhagavata Purana to be born in their first earthly life as which demon brothers?
questions.explanation: In their first birth Jaya and Vijaya are said to incarnate as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu, slain by Varaha and Narasimha.

questions.q: The four Kumara sages, turned away at Vaikuntha's gates, are the mind-born sons of which creator deity?
questions.explanation: The four Kumaras barred at Vaikuntha's gates are the mind-born sons of Brahma.

questions.q: The reclining Vishnu of Vaikuntha is called Padmanabha because of which feature described in the Puranas?
options: A lotus crown upon his head
A lotus pond at his feet
A lotus held in every hand
A lotus growing from his navel
questions.explanation: Padmanabha means 'lotus-naveled,' referring to the lotus that rises from Vishnu's navel bearing Brahma.

questions.q: In Vaishnava theology, the form of Vishnu who eternally reclines in the milk ocean linked to Vaikuntha is often titled by which name?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu reclining on the ocean of milk is called Kshirodakashayi Vishnu in Vaishnava theology.

questions.q: The mace (gada) wielded by Vishnu, lord of Vaikuntha, bears which traditional name?
questions.explanation: Kaumodaki is the name of Vishnu's mace; Nandaka is his sword and Sharanga his bow.

questions.q: The auspicious mark or whorl of hair on Vishnu's chest, depicted in images of the lord of Vaikuntha, is called what?
questions.explanation: The Srivatsa is the auspicious whorl on Vishnu's chest, while the Kaustubha is the gem he wears.

questions.q: The radiant gem worn upon the chest of Vishnu, lord of Vaikuntha, is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha is the celebrated gem worn by Vishnu, obtained from the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: In Vaishnava devotional tradition, attaining the eternal abode of Vaikuntha is often described as a form of which liberation, distinguished from merger into the formless absolute?
options: Residence in the Lord's own realm
questions.explanation: Vaishnava tradition describes attaining Vaikuntha as dwelling eternally in the Lord's own realm and grace, a personal liberation rather than impersonal merger.
